title: Endothermic Dissolution Thermal Controller
description: Closed-loop temperature controller for endothermic dissolution reactions (e.g., ammonium nitrate or potassium nitrate in water). A PWM resistance heater maintains the target dissolution temperature while a relay stirrer homogenises the solution. SD card logs temperature and heater output at 1 Hz. An over-temperature interlock cuts power at 80 °C and halts the system.
objective: Sustain a target dissolution temperature within ±1 °C using PID-controlled heating, with full CSV data logging and over-temperature fault shutdown.
constraints: Arduino Uno, single DS18B20 probe, 12V/20W ceramic heater, relay stirrer, micro-SD logger; safe aqueous reagents only; hardware thermal fuse at 85 °C; max operating setpoint 70 °C.
supporting_fact_titles: Chemical Equilibrium and Le Chatelier's Principle

element_usage_notes.DS18B20 1-Wire Temperature Probe Array: Single probe immersed in bulk solution; polled at 1 Hz for PID feedback.
element_usage_notes.Resistance Heater with PWM Control: PWM duty cycle set by PID output; supplies heat to drive endothermic dissolution.
element_usage_notes.Relay-Switched Magnetic Stirrer: Runs continuously to ensure thermal uniformity and minimise probe stratification error.
element_usage_notes.SD Card Data Logger Module: Writes timestamp, temperature (°C), and PWM value to log.csv each second.

element_interactions.description: PID error drives heater PWM set-point
Heater output logged alongside temperature for energy audit
Raw bulk temperature logged every cycle
Continuous stirring ensures probe reads homogeneous bulk temperature
